findings.title: Pipeline Consolidation Summary
findings.description: Consolidated 40 findings from 7 agents into 31 unique findings. Removed 9 duplicates. Identified 3 cross-agent conflicts.

findings.title: Top Priority Findings
findings.description: Highest priority items based on weighted scoring (user_impact×0.35 + business_value×0.30) / (effort×0.20 + risk×0.15): North Star Metric: Monthly Active Enterprise Users (score=5.125); Primary KPI: Churn Rate for Enterprise Tenants (score=3.129); Measurement Gap: Customer Satisfaction Scores (score=3.129); PlatformOne's Compliance Strengths (score=3.129); Need for Multi-Tenant Data Isolation (score=3.129)

top_priorities.title: North Star Metric: Monthly Active Enterprise Users
Primary KPI: Churn Rate for Enterprise Tenants

findings.title: Cross-Agent Conflict Resolution Report
findings.description: Resolved 3 conflicts between agent findings. intake-001 vs risk-004: Privacy risk detected due to potential non-compliance with SOC 2 Type II requirements related to data isolation.; requirements-002 vs risk-004: Requirement for multi-tenant data isolation conflicts with the identified security risk of current application-level row filtering.; competitive-002 vs requirements-004: Feature gap in data residency controls conflicts with the requirement to implement data residency controls for compliance.

evidence.excerpt: Privacy risk detected due to potential non-compliance with SOC 2 Type II requirements related to data isolation.
Requirement for multi-tenant data isolation conflicts with the identified security risk of current application-level row filtering.
Feature gap in data residency controls conflicts with the requirement to implement data residency controls for compliance.

conflicts.description: Privacy risk detected due to potential non-compliance with SOC 2 Type II requirements related to data isolation.
conflicts.resolution: Implement schema or database-level separation for data isolation to ensure compliance with SOC 2 Type II.
conflicts.reasoning: Addressing the privacy risk by enhancing data isolation will mitigate compliance issues and align with security standards.

conflicts.description: Requirement for multi-tenant data isolation conflicts with the identified security risk of current application-level row filtering.
conflicts.resolution: Remediate the data isolation model by implementing schema or database-level separation to meet compliance requirements.
conflicts.reasoning: This resolution ensures that the requirement for data isolation aligns with security compliance, reducing the risk flagged.

conflicts.description: Feature gap in data residency controls conflicts with the requirement to implement data residency controls for compliance.
conflicts.resolution: Prioritize the implementation of data residency controls to close the feature gap and ensure compliance with GDPR.
conflicts.reasoning: Addressing the feature gap will enhance competitiveness while ensuring compliance with data residency regulations.

summary: Consolidated 40 findings into 31 unique items. Removed 9 duplicates, resolved 3 conflicts. Top priority: North Star Metric: Monthly Active Enterprise Users.
